lesson.menu_desc: Review of Lessons 6-9: routine, scheduling, phone calls and asking for help -- 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Checkpoint 1 -- Your First Month at Work
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maísa de Oliveira Santos | Checkpoint 1 -- Your First Month at Work

listenings.text: Hi Maisa, this is David. Let me tell you about my morning at work. I usually wake up at seven and I get to work at nine. This morning, I have an appointment at ten, so I want to schedule our meeting for the afternoon. Can we meet on Tuesday at three? Also, a client called, but I was not at my desk, so my colleague took a message. One more thing: I do not understand one number in the report. Could you help me later? Thank you very much. See you soon!

Good morning, this is Sarah from the finance team. Here is my morning at work. I always get to work early, at eight o'clock. First, I answer the phone and check my messages. Then I look at my schedule for the day. Today I have a meeting on Wednesday at ten, so I need to confirm the time with David. In the afternoon, I usually help my team with the reports. If someone does not understand a number, I explain it slowly. I love my job. Have a great day!

extra_audio.text: Good morning. I usually get to work at eight. First, I answer the phone. Then I schedule a meeting for the afternoon. Later, I ask David to help me with the report.
